key_insight: H-Res adapts frozen Transformer associative memories by steering activations through a learned residual field, rather than changing weights or adding prompt tokens.
engineering_relevance: This offers a more stable and efficient way to transfer large pretrained models to new tasks while preserving their retrieval capacity and avoiding sequence-length overhead.
method: Treats adaptation as control on the activation manifold.
Learns a state-dependent residual vector field to steer token trajectories toward task-specific attractors.
Preserves the foundation model’s global equilibrium and attention entropy.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a 26% improvement over global weight modification on associative retrieval tasks and claims effective scaling to structured domains.
watch_item: The method may require careful training and verification to ensure the learned steering field generalizes without destabilizing downstream behavior.
paper_title: Parallel Manifold Steering: Efficient Adaptation of Large Associative Memories via Residual Energy Shaping
